title: Caregiver Feature Parity & End-to-End Flow Validation - Functional Test Plan
overview: Comprehensive functional testing of caregiver feature parity with PwD journeys on the AT/AD platform. This test plan validates that caregivers have access to all relevant PwD features including product browsing, detail viewing, recommendation receipt, interest expression, vendor contact, and notifications. Testing includes feature parity comparison, tracking and analytics validation, edge case handling, and QA sign-off verification. The plan ensures caregivers experience seamless platform functionality with clearly communicated exclusions and consistent tracking across all user actions.

inScope: Analytics and activity tracking for caregiver actions
Feature parity verification between caregiver and PwD journeys

testCases.title: Verify caregiver can access product browsing feature (PwD feature parity)
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver account is created and logged in; PwD account exists and has access to product browsing; Product catalog is populated with test data
steps: 1. Log in as caregiver user
2. Navigate to main dashboard or home page
3. Locate 'Browse Products' or 'AT/AD Products' section
4. Click on products browsing section
5. Verify products are displayed in list or grid view
6. Verify product list contains at least 5+ products
7. Verify each product shows basic information (name, category, image)
8. Test pagination or 'Load More' functionality if available
9. Log out and log in as PwD user
10. Access same product browsing section as PwD
11. Compare product list between caregiver and PwD views
12. Verify both views show identical product information
expectedResults: Caregiver can successfully navigate to product browsing section
Product list loads without errors
All products visible to PwD are also visible to caregiver
Product information (name, category, image) is identical in both views
No 'Feature Not Available' messages appear for caregiver
Pagination/lazy-loading works consistently between user types
testCases.postconditions: Caregiver product browsing feature confirmed operational and parity verified with PwD view

testCases.title: Verify caregiver can access product details view (PwD feature parity)
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver and PwD are logged in respectively; Product catalog is populated; Both users have access to same products
steps: 1. Log in as caregiver
2. Navigate to product browsing section
3. Click on first product to view details
4. Verify product details page loads successfully
5. Verify all detail fields display correctly (description, specs, price, availability)
6. Check for images, videos, or multimedia content
7. Verify 'Related Products' or recommendations section if present
8. Log out and repeat steps 2-7 as PwD user
9. Compare all details between caregiver and PwD views
10. Verify information consistency and completeness
expectedResults: Caregiver product details page loads without errors
All product information fields display completely
Product description, specifications, and pricing visible
Images/media content loads properly
Identical information displayed to both caregiver and PwD
'Related Products' section displays same items for both user types
No access restrictions or warning messages for caregiver
testCases.postconditions: Caregiver product details feature verified with full feature parity

testCases.title: Verify caregiver can successfully browse products with filters (edge case)
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver logged in; Product catalog contains diverse products; Filter functionality is available
steps: 1. Log in as caregiver
2. Navigate to product browsing section
3. Locate filter options (if available):
- Category (Mobility, Vision, Hearing, etc.)
- Price range
- Availability status
- Popularity/rating
4. Apply a category filter (e.g., 'Mobility Aids')
5. Verify filtered product list displays
6. Verify all displayed products match selected filter
7. Apply multiple filters simultaneously
8. Verify results match all applied filters
9. Clear filters and verify full product list returns
10. Test with empty filter results (no matching products)
11. Verify appropriate message shows for no results
expectedResults: Filter options are visible and accessible
Caregiver can select single or multiple filters
Filter results accurately reflect selected criteria
Product count updates when filters are applied
Multiple filters can be applied simultaneously
Filter combinations work correctly
'No results' message is clear when no products match
Filters can be cleared to reset product list
testCases.postconditions: Product filtering feature verified functional for caregiver browsing

testCases.title: Verify caregiver search functionality works with relevant keywords
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver logged in; Search functionality is implemented; Product database is populated
steps: 1. Log in as caregiver
2. Locate search box in product section
3. Search for specific product type: 'wheelchair'
4. Verify search results include relevant products
5. Verify search is case-insensitive
6. Search for partial terms: 'mobil' (for mobility aids)
7. Verify autocomplete suggestions appear if available
8. Test search with no results: 'xyz123'
9. Verify 'no results' message displays
10. Test search performance with complex queries
11. Verify search results load within acceptable time
expectedResults: Search box is accessible and functional
Search returns relevant product results
Search is case-insensitive or handles both cases
Autocomplete suggestions enhance user experience
Empty search results show clear message
Search performance is acceptable (< 2 seconds)
Search works with partial keywords
Search results match user intent
testCases.postconditions: Product search functionality verified for caregiver use

testCases.title: Verify caregiver dashboard displays personalized content
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver logged in; Dashboard customization is implemented; User has interacted with platform
steps: 1. Log in as caregiver with history of platform interactions
2. Navigate to caregiver dashboard
3. Verify dashboard displays personalized elements:
- Recommended products based on interests
- Recently viewed products
- Saved/interested products list
- Relevant product categories
4. Verify content is relevant to caregiver's activities
5. Compare caregiver dashboard to PwD dashboard
6. Verify personalization logic is similar for both user types
7. Verify dashboard updates as caregiver takes actions
8. Test with new caregiver (no history):
- Verify default/generic content displays
- Verify prompts guide user through platform discovery
expectedResults: Dashboard displays personalized product recommendations
Recent activity is reflected in dashboard content
Saved/interested products are easily accessible
Recommendations are relevant to caregiver's interests
Dashboard updates when caregiver performs new actions
New caregivers see guided onboarding content
Personalization matches PwD personalization logic
No irrelevant or confusing content on dashboard

AC1_caregiver_feature_access.description: Caregiver access includes all relevant PwD features

assumptions: Caregiver and PwD accounts have identical access to features unless explicitly excluded in documentation
Feature parity requirements apply to all major user-facing features (browse, view, recommend, interest, contact, notify)
Excluded features (if any) are well-documented and approved by stakeholders
Analytics and tracking systems are fully functional and collecting data
Platform usage metrics are properly calculated and reported
All new PwD features go through caregiver applicability evaluation process
QA sign-off is mandatory before any release to production
dependencies: Caregiver registration module must be complete (SCRUM-280 or similar)
PwD registration module must be complete (SCRUM-281)
Product catalog and browsing functionality must be operational
Recommendation engine must be functional and tested
Vendor management system must support caregiver inquiries
Notification system must support caregiver recipients
Analytics and logging infrastructure must be in place
User relationship management (caregiver-PwD) must be working

open_questions_and_clarifications.question: What are the specific features that should be EXCLUDED for caregivers and why?
open_questions_and_clarifications.impact: Affects test cases TC_PARITY_009 and TC_PARITY_010; Need clear list of excluded features

open_questions_and_clarifications.question: Can a caregiver express interest on behalf of a PwD, or only for themselves?
open_questions_and_clarifications.impact: Affects test design and feature parity definitions; Changes interest tracking logic

open_questions_and_clarifications.question: Should caregiver recommendations be based on their own interactions or the PwD's profile (or both)?
open_questions_and_clarifications.impact: Affects test case TC_PARITY_003; Influences recommendation algorithm testing

open_questions_and_clarifications.question: Are there any legal/privacy considerations that mandate certain feature exclusions for caregivers?
open_questions_and_clarifications.impact: Affects feature parity scope; May require legal review for sign-off

open_questions_and_clarifications.question: What are the acceptable pass rates and criteria for QA sign-off?
open_questions_and_clarifications.impact: Affects test case TC_PARITY_011; Defines QA acceptance thresholds

open_questions_and_clarifications.question: Should caregiver-submitted vendor inquiries identify them as caregiver or PwD representative?
open_questions_and_clarifications.impact: Affects vendor communication and tracking; May impact vendor management

open_questions_and_clarifications.question: Are there any platform-specific performance or scalability requirements for caregiver features?
open_questions_and_clarifications.impact: Affects test scope; May require load testing for caregiver transactions

priority_order: Highest tests (TC_PARITY_001, TC_PARITY_002) establish core feature parity and must run first
Low/Medium tests (TC_PARITY_003, TC_PARITY_004, TC_PARITY_005) cover filters, search, and personalization

estimated_effort.critical_path: 8-10 hours for core feature parity tests

test_environment_requirements: Staging environment with production-like data volume
Pre-configured caregiver and PwD test accounts
Product catalog with minimum 50 test products
Admin tools for creating test relationships (caregiver-PwD links)
Mobile device emulators or real devices for responsive testing
regression_areas: Existing PwD feature functionality when caregivers gain access
Data isolation between caregiver and PwD sessions
Relationship management between caregiver and multiple PwDs
Analytics accuracy with mixed user-type metrics
Authentication and authorization for caregiver access
success_criteria: 100% of acceptance criteria are covered by test cases
Analytics tracking is verified for caregiver actions
QA sign-off documentation is completed and approved
No critical or high-priority defects remain open
Regression suite shows no new failures
Caregiver experience is tested alongside PwD flows
Feature exclusions are documented and communicated

metadata.coverage: 100% acceptance criteria coverage with comprehensive feature parity and QA validation scenarios
nextSteps: Review test plan with QA lead and stakeholders
Clarify open questions with product team
Prepare test environment and test data
Begin test execution based on priority order
Execute QA sign-off process before release
